title: Mildly Spicy Syrian Chicken Pilaf
description: A salty and aerated Syrian dish featuring coconut flakes, perfect for a post-workout meal, with anti-inflammatory properties. It is cooked in a paella pan with a whisk.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken breast and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the basmati rice and stir to combine with the chicken and spices.
Add 4 cups of water to the paella pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the paella pan, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the coconut flakes and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove the paella pan from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Use a whisk to fluff the pilaf and serve hot.
